figures.caption: Figure 12. Activation coverage: number of images activating each concept.
Figure 11. Activation density: number of active concepts per image.
Figure 19. CUB class-level visualizations.

Figure 1. Overview of EZPC. CLIP image and text embeddings are projected into a shared concept space using a learnable matrix A. The projected representations cx and ck provide (i) concept-based explanations via a Hadamard product and (ii) class logits via a dot-product in concept space. Training jointly optimizes a matching loss and a reconstruction loss to preserve CLIP’s predictive behavior.

Figure 4. Class-level Concept Explanations. For each class, we average concept activations over nine sampled images. EZPC produces coherent class signatures, highlighting concepts that characterize each category.

Figure 3. Image-level Explanations. For each image, EZPC displays the top-10 activated concepts that contribute most to the zero-shot prediction. The highlighted concepts closely correspond to salient visual characteristics of the input images.
Figure 20. Places365 class-level visualizations.
Figure 9. Alignment distribution between learned concept directions Aj and original CLIP directions Φj . High alignment values indicate semantic consistency between the learned space and CLIP.
Figure 10. PCA comparison between CLIP concept space (Φ) and learned space (A). The learned space is more compact yet preserves the structural layout of concept groups.
Figure 15. CUB image-level explanations.
Figure 16. Places365 image-level explanations.
Figure 21. Concept clustering examples across datasets.
Figure 8. Causal intervention analysis. Removing the top-10 influential concepts produces a substantially larger drop in the predicted class logit than removing 10 random concepts.
Figure 7. Faithfulness distributions for n=1, 3, 5, 10. Removing more highly ranked concepts yields consistently larger drops in model confidence.

Figure 5. Concept Clustering Results. For each concept, we retrieve the nine images with the highest activation. Clusters from ImageNet100 and Places365 show coherent semantic structure, indicating that EZPC learns interpretable concept directions.
Figure 17. CIFAR-100 class-level visualizations.
Figure 14. ImageNet-100 image-level explanations.
Figure 13. CIFAR-100 image-level explanations.
Figure 18. ImageNet-100 class-level visualizations.

Figure 2. Qualitative comparison of image-level explanations for different λ values. For λ = 1, EZPC produces semantically consistent concept activations. For larger values (e.g., λ = 100), unrelated concepts appear among the top activations.
